activities_projects.title: Federal vs Unitary Comparison Chart
activities_projects.objective: Analyse differences between federal and unitary systems
steps: Create two columns titled Federal Government and Unitary Government
Write at least four characteristics of each system
Add examples of countries for each type
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical understanding of different governance systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Power Distribution Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand how powers are distributed in India
steps: List five subjects under Union List
List five subjects under State List
List five subjects under Concurrent List
Explain why such distribution is necessary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands constitutional distribution of legislative powers

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Linguistic States
activities_projects.objective: Examine the impact of linguistic reorganisation
steps: Choose one state created due to linguistic reorganisation
Write about the language spoken in that state
Explain how linguistic states improved administration
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the political importance of linguistic states
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Centre–State Relations Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the evolution of Centre-State relations
steps: Describe how Centre-State relations functioned before 1990
Explain the impact of coalition governments
Discuss how regional parties strengthened federalism
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of political changes affecting federalism
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Federal Countries Map Study
steps: Mark India on the map
Identify and mark three other federal countries
Write one feature of federalism for each country
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises global examples of federal systems

activities_projects.title: Language Policy Debate
steps: Write arguments supporting the use of Hindi as official language
Write arguments supporting the continued use of English
Give your conclusion on the importance of language flexibility
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking about language policy

activities_projects.title: Local Government Survey
steps: Identify your local governing body (panchayat or municipality)
List three functions performed by this body
Explain how local governments help people directly

activities_projects.title: Structure of Panchayati Raj
steps: Draw a diagram showing Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti and Zilla Parishad
Write one responsibility of each level
Explain how these bodies interact
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the multi-level structure of rural governance

activities_projects.title: Scheduled Languages Research
steps: List five scheduled languages in India
Identify the language spoken in your region
Explain how linguistic diversity affects governance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands India's cultural and linguistic diversity

activities_projects.title: Federalism Reflection Essay
activities_projects.objective: Reflect on the importance of federalism in India
steps: Write a short essay explaining federalism
Explain why federalism is important in a diverse country like India
Give examples from Indian politics
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops deeper understanding of federal principles
